ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON CROSS-DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION SERVICES INC.'S DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PARAGRAPH 24 IN THE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT MUST SPECIFY WHICH REPRESENTATIONS THE CROSS-DEFENDANT KNEW WERE FALSE WHEN MADE. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER AND CROSS-DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO STRIKE THE OPPOSITION PAPERS IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. (ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.) REPLY MEMO IN SUPP OF DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION SERVICES INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES DAMAGE AND THE SHAM DOCTRINE DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE THERE IS NO INCONSISTENCY BETWEEN THE ORIGINAL AND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CAUSE OF ACTION STILL LACKS THE REQUISITE PARTICULARITY. DEFENDANT HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH THE SOURCE OF THE REPRESENTATION, WHETHER IT WAS WRITTEN OR ORAL, AND WHEN IT OCCURRED. THE ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPH 14 PERTAIN TO WHEN THE TRANSPORTATION TROUBLES AROSE, NOT THE ORIGINAL MISREPRESENTATIONS. THERE IS NO PROPER MOTION TO STRIKE ON CALENDAR. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2014 Set For Trial On Sep-14-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MALCOLM LEADER-PICONE, TEL. NO. (510) 444-2404 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ROBERT C. SMITH, TEL. NO. 294-1465 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION SERVICES INC.'s DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION OF THE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is overruled. Paragraphs 15-24 of the amended cross-complaint allege the misrepresentations with sufficient particularity. Paragraphs 15 and 23 refer to representations of present/past facts, i.e., that plaintiff "had" a pick up program. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2014 Continued To Aug-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION SERVICES INC.'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT/MOTION TO STRIKE is continued to August 28, 2014 to provide Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant the opportunity to file an additional reply brief in response to Defendant/Cross-Complainant's late-served opposition. Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant may file a substantive reply brief not exceeding ten pages up to five calendar days before the new hearing date. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling on CROSS-DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION SERVICES INC.'s DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant's demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Paragraph 24 in the Second Amended Cross-Complaint must specify which representations the Cross-Defendant knew were false when made. The Motion to Strike is off calendar in light of the ruling on the demurrer and Cross-Defendant's request to strike the opposition papers is off calendar as moot. (ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.) Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Continued To Apr-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MALCOLM LEADER-PICONE OF BARTLETT, LEADER-PICONE & YOUNG LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 444-2404 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ROBERT SMITH OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MARY CATHERINE WALSH, TEL. NO. 294-1465 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT UNPARALLELED PRODUCTIONS INC;

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled as to the breach of contract cause of action. Defendant sufficiently alleges damage and the sham doctrine does not apply because there is no inconsistency between the original and amended cross-complaint. The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The cause of action still lacks the requisite particularity. Defendant has leave to allege in good faith the source of the representation, whether it was written or oral, and when it occurred. The allegations in paragraph 14 pertain to when the transportation troubles arose, not the original misrepresentations. There is no proper motion to strike on calendar. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Continued To Jan-08-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-21-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Cross-complainant has leave to amend to allege its contract damages more clearly. Paragraph 7 of the Cross-Complaint alleges the Cross-complainant did not make payment on the contract. Cross-complainant has leave to allege the fraud cause of action with more particularity. The demurrer to the negligence cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. That claim merely restates the breach of contract cause of action. Cross-complainant does not highlight a legal duty that cross-defendant breached that is independent of the contract. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/MJM)
